locations.name: Riverside Park
locations.parking_details: Free surface lot off Riverside Park Way; overflow street parking available on weekends.

locations.name: Riverside Community Center
locations.parking_details: Small free lot behind the center; additional parking along Oak Lane.

locations.name: Riverside Adventure Playground
locations.parking_details: Shared with Riverside Park main lot; look for Playground Entrance signs.

locations.name: Downtown Children's Museum
locations.parking_details: Validated parking available in the Market Street Garage; first 2 hours $5 with museum validation.

locations.name: Maple Grove Public Library
locations.parking_details: Underground garage with 3 hours free for library visitors; meter parking on Elm Street.

locations.name: Willow Creek Park
locations.parking_details: Two small free lots off Willow Creek Rd; street parking fills quickly on weekends.

locations.name: Sunset Amphitheater
locations.parking_details: Paid event parking lot ($10–$20 depending on event); limited neighborhood street parking.

locations.name: Pine Hill Recreation Center
locations.parking_details: Large free lot behind the gym; accessible spaces near main entrance.

locations.name: Lakeside Botanical Garden
locations.parking_details: Paid lot ($5 flat rate); free bike parking near the main gate.

locations.name: Valley View Mall
locations.parking_details: Multi-level free parking garages A–C; family parking spaces near entrances.

locations.name: Oakridge Elementary School
locations.parking_details: School lot open for public events only; follow event signage.

locations.name: Little Oaks Play Café
locations.parking_details: Metered street parking on Maple Ave; free 2-hour parking on nearby side streets.

locations.name: Bright Steps Gymnastics
locations.parking_details: Shared free lot in front of the building; additional parking on the south side.

locations.name: Adventure Trampoline Park
locations.parking_details: Large free parking lot surrounding the building.

locations.name: Meadowbrook Family Farm
locations.parking_details: Free gravel lot near farm entrance; follow signs for event parking.

locations.name: Winterfair Grounds at Lakeside
locations.parking_details: Event parking lots P1 and P2 ($10 cash or card); rideshare drop-off at Gate 2.

locations.name: Historic Main Street
locations.parking_details: Several paid public lots along Main Street; limited free street parking on side streets.

locations.name: Skyline Plaza
locations.parking_details: Underground paid parking; validation available at select restaurants and venues.

memberships.name: Family Fun Basic Membership
memberships.description: Perfect for families who attend a few events each season. Includes discounted tickets and a handful of free family days throughout the year.
benefits: 4 free family event admissions per year
10% discount on all regular event tickets
Early access to seasonal event registration
Members-only email newsletter with weekly highlights

memberships.name: Family Adventure Plus Membership
memberships.description: Our most popular family plan with more free events, bigger discounts, and extra perks for busy families who love to explore.
benefits: 8 free family event admissions per year
15% discount on all regular event tickets
Priority booking for workshops & camps
Free parking at select partner venues
Member pricing on up to 4 guest tickets per event

memberships.name: Family Explorer Premium Membership
memberships.description: Best value for families who attend events regularly all year. Maximizes free events and access to special members-only experiences.
benefits: 12 free family event admissions per year
20% discount on all regular event tickets
Unlimited free admission to select indoor play mornings
Exclusive members-only holiday preview night
Waived registration fees for up to 3 workshops per year
Priority phone support for booking changes

memberships.name: Park & Play Family Pass
memberships.description: Budget-friendly family pass focused on outdoor parks, playground meetups, and seasonal park festivals.
benefits: 6 free outdoor park events per year
10% discount on park-based workshops
Early registration for summer park series
Members-only playground meetup invitations

memberships.name: Individual Weekend Pass
memberships.description: Ideal for grandparents, caregivers, or parents who occasionally join weekend events.
benefits: 3 free individual admissions to weekend events per year
10% discount on personal tickets
Access to members-only early bird offers

memberships.name: Individual All-Access Membership
memberships.description: For individual caregivers who attend events frequently and want maximum flexibility and savings.
benefits: 10 free individual admissions per year
25% discount on additional tickets
No service fees on ticket exchanges
Exclusive invitations to preview events

activities.title: Riverside Park Bug Safari
activities.subtitle: Guided nature walk for curious kids
activities.short_description: Join a naturalist-led walk to hunt for beetles, butterflies, and more along the Riverside Park trails.
activities.description: Bring your magnifying glass and curiosity for this kid-friendly bug safari at Riverside Park. Families will explore easy, stroller-friendly paths while our guide helps kids spot beetles, butterflies, ladybugs, and other tiny critters. Short stops include hands-on observation with bug viewers and simple nature games. Perfect for ages 5–8; younger siblings welcome with supervision.

activities.title: Willow Creek Nature Scavenger Hunt
activities.subtitle: Free outdoor adventure for elementary-age kids
activities.short_description: Explore Willow Creek Park with a guided scavenger hunt designed for ages 5–10.
activities.description: Families receive a colorful scavenger hunt card featuring leaves, birds, park landmarks, and sounds to discover around Willow Creek Park. A host will lead a gentle loop walk with check-in stops for group games and nature facts. Kids who complete the card can pick a small nature-themed prize at the end. Dress for outdoor play.

activities.title: Morning Makers Indoor Play & Craft Time
activities.subtitle: Cozy indoor play with guided art projects
activities.short_description: Drop-in play, sensory bins, and a simple take-home craft at Little Oaks Play Café.
activities.description: Ease into Sunday with an indoor play session at Little Oaks Play Café. Kids can rotate between open play structures, sensory bins, and a hosted craft table with a new project each week (think collages, painting, or simple building). A quieter corner with dimmed lighting is available for kids who need a break. Coffee and snacks available for purchase.

activities.title: Willow Creek Park Picnic & Play
activities.subtitle: Relaxed afternoon in the park for the whole family
activities.short_description: Spread a blanket, enjoy lawn games, and let kids explore the playground with new friends.
activities.description: Spend an easy Sunday afternoon at Willow Creek Park. The event area features picnic blankets, hula hoops, bubbles, and family-friendly lawn games set up near the main playground. A host will lead a short movement game and group parachute play half-way through the afternoon. Feel free to bring your own snacks or a picnic lunch.

activities.title: Family Storybook Theater Show
activities.subtitle: Interactive evening performance based on favorite picture books
activities.short_description: Actors bring classic children's stories to life with music, movement, and audience participation.
activities.description: Gather for an early evening performance at Sunset Amphitheater where a small cast adapts beloved picture books into a playful live show. Expect sing-alongs, call-and-response moments, and gentle audience participation designed with younger kids in mind. Concessions are available on-site, and blankets or low lawn chairs are recommended for hillside seating.

activities.title: Puppet Pals Mini Show
activities.subtitle: Budget-friendly early evening puppet theater
activities.short_description: A 40-minute puppet show with familiar songs and simple stories, perfect before bedtime.
activities.description: Head to the Downtown Children's Museum theater space for a cozy puppet performance. The show includes short vignettes built around everyday routines—getting dressed, visiting the park, and bedtime—told with colorful puppets and live music. Families are invited to stay for a brief puppet meet-and-greet and photo opportunity after the show.

activities.title: Intro Robotics Saturday Lab
activities.subtitle: Hands-on building and coding for ages 10–12
activities.short_description: Kids build simple robots and learn basic coding with friendly instructors.
activities.description: This small-group workshop introduces kids to robotics through guided challenges. Participants work in pairs to assemble basic robots using kid-friendly kits and then program them to complete obstacle courses. No prior coding experience is required; all laptops and materials are provided. Parents may drop off after check-in or stay in the lobby.

activities.title: Creative Writing Quest Workshop
activities.subtitle: Imaginative story-building for upper elementary writers
activities.short_description: A Saturday morning writing adventure with prompts, games, and a mini book to take home.
activities.description: Young writers dive into character creation, world-building, and story structure through guided activities at Maple Grove Public Library. The session includes group brainstorms, individual writing time, and a chance to share stories with peers. Each participant leaves with a stapled mini-book of their work and a list of prompts to keep the creativity flowing.

activities.title: Math Puzzles & Brain Games Camp Day
activities.subtitle: Logical challenges and team games for ages 10–12
activities.short_description: A single-day camp packed with math puzzles, escape-room style challenges, and brainteasers.
activities.description: At Oakridge Elementary School, kids rotate through stations featuring hands-on math challenges, logic puzzles, and collaborative problem-solving games. Activities emphasize curiosity and teamwork rather than speed or grades. The day includes snack breaks and an outside movement break (weather permitting).

activities.title: Little Explorers Weekly STEAM Club
activities.subtitle: Hands-on science and art for preschoolers
activities.short_description: A recurring after-school class blending simple science experiments and creative art projects.
activities.description: Each week at the Downtown Children's Museum, preschoolers explore a new theme such as colors, magnets, or weather through age-appropriate experiments and process art. Classes include circle time, a guided activity, and open-ended exploration stations. Caregivers may participate or observe from the side.

activities.title: Tiny Tumblers Gymnastics Class
activities.subtitle: Weekly movement class for ages 3–4
activities.short_description: Beginner tumbling, balance, and coordination exercises in a soft-play gym.
activities.description: At Bright Steps Gymnastics, little ones practice rolling, balancing on low beams, and jumping on toddler-safe trampolines under coach supervision. The class emphasizes confidence and gross motor skills over competition. Parents or caregivers stay on the floor to support their child.

activities.title: Preschool Play & Learn Storytime Series
activities.subtitle: Weekly interactive stories and songs at the library
activities.short_description: Librarians lead themed stories, rhymes, and playtime for 3–4-year-olds and caregivers.
activities.description: This ongoing weekday series at Maple Grove Public Library combines read-aloud stories, fingerplays, songs, and a brief free-play segment with simple toys. Each week features a new theme such as animals, feelings, or transportation. Families are welcome to stay afterward to browse books together.

activities.title: Riverside Playground Pop-Up Playdate
activities.subtitle: Casual meetup with bubbles and chalk
activities.short_description: Drop by the Riverside Adventure Playground for hosted play activities and a relaxed meetup.
activities.description: Look for the event banner near the slide tower at Riverside Adventure Playground. Hosts provide sidewalk chalk, bubbles, simple obstacle courses, and a short group game midway through. This is a flexible drop-in event—come and go as you please. Families often bring snacks and water to share at the picnic tables.

activities.title: Riverside Family Storytime in the Park
activities.subtitle: Outdoor stories and songs by the river
activities.short_description: A librarian-led storytime with music and movement on the lawn near Riverside Community Center.
activities.description: Bring a blanket and join the Maple Grove Library team for stories, rhymes, and simple movement songs at Riverside Park. The session ends with a short free-play period with scarves and bubbles. In case of bad weather, storytime moves indoors to Riverside Community Center.

activities.title: Old Town Holiday Parade & Tree Lighting
activities.subtitle: Daytime community parade with free parking
activities.short_description: Floats, marching bands, and costumed characters along Historic Main Street, ending with a tree lighting.
activities.description: Kick off the holiday week with a festive daytime parade through Old Town. Expect local school bands, community groups, and costumed characters handing out candy canes. The route ends at the town square for carols and a tree lighting ceremony. Designated free parking lots are available a short walk from the parade route; plan to arrive early.

activities.title: Lakeside Holiday Lights Walk
activities.subtitle: Evening lights display around the garden paths
activities.short_description: Stroll through Lakeside Botanical Garden’s illuminated paths with music, cocoa stands, and photo spots.
activities.description: Experience thousands of twinkling lights, glowing animal sculptures, and gently themed music as you walk the paved paths at Lakeside Botanical Garden. Warm drinks and snacks are available for purchase, and several designated photo areas make it easy to capture memories. Timed-entry tickets help keep crowds comfortable for families with younger children.

activities.title: Sensory-Friendly Museum Morning
activities.subtitle: Lower lights and quieter sounds at the Children's Museum
activities.short_description: A specially designed indoor session with reduced crowds, softer sounds, and designated quiet zones.
activities.description: Downtown Children's Museum opens early for families who prefer a sensory-adjusted experience. Exhibits operate with reduced sound levels, dimmer lighting, and limited attendance. A quiet room with beanbags and noise-canceling headphones is available throughout the session. Staff receive additional training to support neurodivergent children and their families.

activities.title: Quiet Play Hour at Little Oaks
activities.subtitle: Smaller group, softer sounds, same fun space
activities.short_description: A low-capacity indoor play session with calming music and extra staff support.
activities.description: Little Oaks Play Café limits capacity and turns down the background music for this sensory-friendly hour. Lights are softened where possible, and families may request further adjustments on arrival. The climbing structures, pretend play areas, and reading nooks remain open, with an optional small craft table for kids who prefer seated activities.
